title: Slim the router: cut what AGENTS.md already says, keep every pin
action: Per D5/D6. AGENTS.md is auto-loaded into every session, so anything skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md restates from it is paid twice on every cold route. Cut ONLY the unpinned duplication, and only where AGENTS.md genuinely already carries the rule: Priority Rules 2/3/4/10/11/13 (AGENTS.md critical rules 6/7/1/10/11/12), the Runtime Files section (AGENTS.md Working files, which is the fuller list), the Hook Response Protocol (AGENTS.md Guardrails), the Red Flags list (~85% identical to AGENTS.md's), the Headless section, and the bulk of the Onboarding section. Each cut leaves ONE line naming the rule and pointing at where it lives in full — a pointer, never a silent deletion; the reader must still learn the rule exists. Critical patterns are currently stated twice inside SKILL.md itself (Session Scout and Priority Rule 7): keep one. Detail worth preserving that has no home moves into skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md under a clearly named section, and every new pointer this cell creates must pass rc-2's check — that is why rc-2 is a dependency and not a nicety. HARD CONSTRAINT: the ten pins in D6 survive character-for-character, and the pinned Modes and Lanes region plus the Routing table and the Four Gates questions are ROUTE-CRITICAL and stay. okf_instructions_fence.mjs also runs live over skills/**/*.md: any surviving line naming docs/specs/ or critical-patterns.md must keep a bundle-branch marker on the SAME line. Report the before and after byte size of SKILL.md in the trace. Then the D8 regen chain: release_manifest --write plus self-onboard.

truths: All seven REQUIRED pins survive character-for-character; all three BANNED strings stay absent.
The Routing table, the Modes and Lanes region, and the four gate questions are intact.
Every cut section leaves a one-line pointer naming the rule and its home — no rule vanishes without a trace.
Every pointer this cell creates resolves under scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs.
Critical patterns are stated once in SKILL.md, not twice.
okf_instructions_fence.mjs is green: every retired-path line still carries its same-line bundle marker.
The full node scripts/run_verify.mjs chain is green.
The trace records SKILL.md's byte size before and after.
artifacts: The slimmed skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md, any moved sections in routing-and-contracts.md, refreshed renders and manifest
prohibitions: Never delete a rule without leaving a pointer to where it now lives.
Never reword a pinned string to make prose flow better.
Never cut the Routing table, the Modes and Lanes region, or the gate questions.
Never move prose into a reference without verifying the pointer resolves.

trace.outcome: Slimmed skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md from 35815 to 29115 bytes (-18.7%, 231->199 lines) by cutting only what AGENTS.md — auto-loaded into every session — genuinely already carries. Every cut left a one-line pointer naming the rule and its home; all 11 headings survive, so a reader scanning headings still learns each rule exists. Moved into references/routing-and-contracts.md under new named sections: the 11 onboarding status bullets (Onboarding Protocol), hive law 10 in full, and the headless contract. THE ORCHESTRATOR'S OWN ANALYSIS WAS WRONG ON TWO ROWS AND THE WORKER CAUGHT IT: the cell brief mapped Priority Rules 10 and 11 to AGENTS.md critical rules 10 and 11, but those two AGENTS.md rules themselves end with 'Full rule: bee-hive skill, critical rule 10/11' — following the brief would have built a circular pointer with the full rule living nowhere. Both were routed to routing-and-contracts.md instead so the chain terminates. Three further items proved NOT carried by AGENTS.md and were kept verbatim rather than cut: .bee/capture-queue.jsonl (added to the reference's File Quick Reference), the bee decision review session-end nudge, and four router-only red flags. Honest correction to D7's projection: the saving is ~1675 tokens per cold route, not the ~5k estimated — cutting further would have meant removing route-critical prose or touching the pinned region.
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":"scripts/test_gate_bypass_doctrine.mjs:137-200 and skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_misc.mjs:1839-1844 (the ten verbatim pins); scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs (rc-2's gate, the prerequisite this cell was sequenced behind); scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs (same-line bundle marker on every surviving retired-path line); the full 90-suite run_verify chain.","tests_added_or_changed":"No test added. This cell's safety net was built as its own prerequisite: rc-2's scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs exists precisely because moving prose into a reference is the migration whose instruction layer rots silently. That gate went from 96 to 101 pointers checked across this cell — it validated the five pointers this surgery created, which is the exact failure mode D5 sequenced it to catch.","red_failure_evidence":"Two independent standing red-evidences, both known to bite rather than assumed to. (1) The ten pins were enumerated character-for-character before any edit (CONTEXT D6) so a reword drives a REQUIRED count to 0 and fails test_gate_bypass_doctrine.mjs; measured before and after, all ten counts are identical (REQUIRED 1/1/2/1/1/1/4, BANNED 0/0/0), and the pinned Modes and Lanes region is byte-untouched — the diff is two hunks, nothing between lines 50 and 175 changed. (2) test_skill_pointers.mjs was proven in rc-2 to DETECT both a missing target file and a missing named section, via negative controls that run on every verify; it was then pointed at this cell's five new pointers and reports 0 broken. A dangling pointer created by this surgery would have turned it red — that is the whole reason it shipped first.","verification_run":"Orchestrator re-ran independently, not the worker's word: wc -c -> 29115 (from 35815); grep -cF of all ten pins -> REQUIRED 1/1/2/1/1/1/4, BANNED 0/0/0; grep '^## ' -> all 11 headings present; the circular-pointer claim independently confirmed by grep on AGENTS.md:52-53, which do end with 'Full rule: bee-hive skill, critical rule 10/11'; node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs -> 101 pointer(s) checked, 0 broken; node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 90 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=69056ms, every suite green."}
trace.verify_output: Orchestrator re-ran independently. skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md: 35815 -> 29115 bytes (-6700, -18.7%), 231 -> 199 lines. All ten pins verified by direct grep -cF, before and after identical: REQUIRED 1/1/2/1/1/1/4, BANNED 0/0/0. All 11 headings survive (Triage first, Onboarding, Session Scout, Routing, Modes and Lanes, The Four Gates, Priority Rules, Runtime Files, Hook Response Protocol, Headless, Red Flags) — a reader scanning headings still sees every rule exists. test_skill_pointers: 101 pointer(s) checked, 0 broken (was 96; the 5 pointers this cell created all resolve, named sections included). PASS run_verify: 90 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=69056ms.
